records.prompt_text: build a CLI command that reads all _learnings.md files, extracts entries newer than 7 days, and produces a summary report
records.judge_reason: This task requires designing and building a complete CLI tool with file system traversal, markdown parsing, date extraction/comparison logic, and report generation — a multi-component implementation spanning 50+ lines that warrants S4, not a simple single-file edit at S2.

records.prompt_text: write a migration script that reads the old JSON config format and converts it to the new TOML manifest format with validation
records.judge_reason: This task requires multi-step reasoning: parsing JSON, understanding two different format schemas, writing conversion logic with field mapping, adding validation, and producing a complete script — easily 10-50+ lines with dependency chains, making S3 the appropriate tier, not S2.

records.prompt_text: implement the token_snapshot parser so it handles both 5-minute and 1-hour cache windows separately and produces per-model cost attribution
records.judge_reason: Implementing a parser with dual cache window handling and per-model cost attribution requires architectural design, multiple data structures, and cross-component logic that far exceeds a simple single-file edit (S2), warranting S4-level treatment.

records.prompt_text: write integration tests for the brain_cli hook that simulate UserPromptSubmit events and verify decisions.jsonl output format
records.judge_reason: Writing integration tests that simulate events, hook into CLI behavior, and verify structured output format requires multi-step reasoning, understanding of the brain_cli architecture, test harness setup, and likely cross-file coordination — this is S4 complexity, not a simple single-file edit.

records.prompt_text: read every file under Desktop/atelier, generate a feature-parity PR for Ink Finder that reuses Atelier's theme system, marketplace UI, and inbox pattern, with passing tests
records.judge_reason: This task requires reading an entire codebase (every file under Desktop/atelier), performing cross-repo architecture analysis, generating a multi-file PR with feature parity across theme system, marketplace UI, and inbox pattern, plus passing tests — this is a production-scale overnight run requiring S5's long-context synthesis and 1M+ context window.

records.prompt_text: read the entire UE Knowledge corpus (18 files, 46K+ lines), cross-reference with Sworder Bible animation chapter, and produce a verified gap analysis of what's documented vs what's implemented in the codebase
records.judge_reason: This task requires ingesting 46K+ lines across 18+ files, cross-referencing multiple documents, and producing a synthesized gap analysis — a massive long-context synthesis task requiring S5, not S2 which handles single-file edits of 3-10 lines.

records.prompt_text: read every page of the Ribbe Universal Framework PDF, verify each of the 8 propositions against current physics literature, annotate internal consistency, and produce a structured peer-review document
records.judge_reason: Reading every page of a PDF, cross-referencing 8 propositions against current physics literature, annotating internal consistency, and producing a structured peer-review document requires massive context processing and long-form synthesis that demands S5's 1M context and 64K output capacity.

records.prompt_text: full ecosystem audit: read all 29 skill SKILL.md files, all memory files, all hook configurations, all CLAUDE.md files — produce a unified dependency graph, identify circular references, dead configs, and orphaned files, with per-item fix proposals
records.judge_reason: Auditing 29+ files across multiple configuration types, building a unified dependency graph, detecting circular references and orphaned files, then producing per-item fix proposals is a massive long-context synthesis task requiring S5's 1M context window and 64K output capacity — S4's 32K context is likely insufficient for this scale.
